pkg/pagination/v2.purpose: Generic cursor-based pagination library providing Cursor (time+ID composite, base64-encoded), Result[T], Paginator[T] interface, and CollectAll helper. All domain list queries needing stable keyset pagination must use these types — never offset-based pagination.

patterns.name: Item interface for cursor derivation
patterns.description: Any type passed to NewResult[T] must implement Item (single method: Cursor() Cursor). NewResult derives the next-page cursor from the last element's Cursor() call automatically.
patterns.example: func (e MyEntity) Cursor() pagination.Cursor { return pagination.NewCursor(e.CreatedAt, e.ID) }

patterns.name: Cursor encoding: base64(RFC3339,ID)
patterns.description: Cursors are opaque to callers. Internal format is base64-encoded '<RFC3339 UTC>,<ID>'. Always use NewCursor/Encode/DecodeCursor — never construct the base64 string manually. Time is always normalised to UTC. SplitN with limit=2 allows commas in IDs.
patterns.example: c := pagination.NewCursor(t, id); encoded := c.Encode(); decoded, err := pagination.DecodeCursor(encoded)

patterns.name: Paginator[T] via NewPaginator
patterns.description: Wrap any page-fetch function as a Paginator[T] using NewPaginator. The function receives a *Cursor (nil on first call) and returns Result[T] with Items and NextCursor.

patterns.name: CollectAll with MAX_SAFE_ITER cap
patterns.description: CollectAll iterates pages until NextCursor is nil, capped at MAX_SAFE_ITER (10_000) iterations. Returns partial results alongside any mid-iteration error. Never use for unbounded production loads without awareness of the cap.

patterns.name: nil NextCursor signals last page
patterns.description: Result.NextCursor == nil means no further pages. NewResult sets NextCursor to nil when items slice is empty. Callers must check this before issuing another page request.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Item interface (Cursor() Cursor) and Paginator[T] interface with NewPaginator constructor wrapping a closure.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding methods to Item breaks all existing implementors; keep it to Cursor() Cursor only.

key_file_guides.role: Cursor value type with base64 encode/decode, MarshalText/UnmarshalText, Validate, and EncodePtr helper. Time is always UTC.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not change cursorDelimiter or encoding format — existing encoded cursors stored by clients will break. SplitN limit=2 intentionally allows commas in IDs.

key_file_guides.role: Result[T] struct and NewResult[T Item] constructor. NextCursor is derived from the last item's Cursor() method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewResult requires T to implement Item. When the caller controls the next cursor manually, build Result[T] directly without NewResult.

key_file_guides.role: CollectAll iterates a Paginator until exhausted or MAX_SAFE_ITER (10_000) reached. Returns (partialItems, err) on mid-iteration error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Silently stops at 10_000 items without error. Callers must handle both return values — partial results are intentionally returned alongside errors.

anti_patterns: Implementing offset-based pagination — this package is cursor-only; offset logic belongs elsewhere
Manually constructing the cursor string (e.g. base64-encoding a custom format) instead of using NewCursor/Encode
Calling NewResult[T] without T implementing Item — compile error, but watch for wrapper types that forget the interface
Ignoring the (items, error) dual return from CollectAll — partial results are intentionally returned alongside errors
Storing decoded Cursor fields (Time/ID) externally and reconstructing — treat Cursor as opaque; always re-encode via Encode()

decisions.decision: Cursor encodes both time and ID (composite key)
decisions.rationale: Time alone is not unique across rows; the ID disambiguates ties, enabling stable keyset pagination even when multiple records share the same timestamp.
Prevents runaway memory usage if a Paginator implementation never returns nil NextCursor. Tests explicitly assert the cap is honoured.

decisions.decision: Paginator[T] as an interface wrapping a function via NewPaginator
decisions.rationale: Allows test paginators to be constructed as closures without implementing a named type, keeping test code concise.

code_examples.scenario: Implement a domain list method that returns paginated results with a next cursor
